leagueAdjustment: TheLeague uses tiered PPR (TE 1.0, WR 0.5, RB 0.25) and 6pt passing TDs — QBs worth more, WRs/RBs worth less for receptions vs RSP baseline
shelfLife: 3 years from draft class (expires after 2027 season)

A.description: See field early, develop into productive starters within 2-3 years

B.description: Short-term depth obstacle but starter talent; contributor talent with immediate fit

C.description: Contributor potential on crowded depth chart; situational talent with good immediate fit

D.description: Stash — long-term developmental talent or reserve

F.description: UDFAs watched but not formally scored — bump 35-50 spots if signed

typeSymbols.Z: Sleeper — lesser-known talent with skills if earns opportunity

valueSystem.Under X: RSP values player X picks higher than ADP (bargain — add X to rank for latest you can get him)
valueSystem.Over X: RSP values player X picks lower than ADP (market overvalues — subtract X from rank for where others take him)

players.name: Ashton Jeanty
players.notes: Dynamic mover with excellent vision, slippery power, and stamina. Finishes plays downfield with same energy he started. Uncommon.

players.name: Luther Burden III
players.notes: RSP's top WR talent. Under 8 means significant market discount. Bears offense with Caleb Williams.

players.name: Travis Hunter
players.notes: Two-way player. RSP pre-draft had him as WR9 on talent but post-draft bumped to overall #3 on situation.

players.name: Quinshon Judkins
players.notes: High ceiling. Cleveland landing spot.

players.name: Kaleb Johnson
players.notes: High ceiling. Pittsburgh.

players.name: Cam Skattebo
players.notes: Slight market discount. High ceiling.

players.name: TreVeyon Henderson
players.notes: High ceiling. New England.

players.name: Matthew Golden
players.notes: Green Bay — great landing spot for WR development.

players.name: Emeka Egbuka
players.notes: Tampa Bay.

players.name: Cam Ward
players.notes: Safe starter and priority selection per RSP. High ceiling. 6pt passing TDs in TheLeague makes him more valuable than RSP baseline.

players.name: Jayden Higgins
players.notes: Houston. CJ Stroud connection.

players.name: Tetairoa McMillan
players.notes: Slight overpay risk. High risk/reward. Carolina situation uncertain.

players.name: Colston Loveland
players.notes: High ceiling TE. Full PPR in TheLeague (1.0 for TE) makes him match RSP's valuation.

players.name: Omarion Hampton
players.notes: Market overpaying by ~6 picks. High ceiling but crowded.

players.name: R.J. Harvey
players.notes: Denver.

players.name: Jalen Royals
players.notes: MAJOR SLEEPER. Under 12 + Underrated tag. Kansas City. Waldman thinks market is sleeping on him by a full round.

players.name: Mason Taylor

players.name: Harold Fannin Jr.
players.notes: MAJOR SLEEPER. Under 10 + Underrated + High ceiling. Cleveland.

players.name: Jalen Milroe
players.notes: BEST UPSIDE QB in the class per Waldman. Under 14 = massive bargain. Patience play behind Darnold. 6pt passing TDs in TheLeague amplifies his value. RSP says 'the qualities he possesses are the most promising of all the passers in this class.'

players.name: Jaylin Noel
players.notes: Houston.

players.name: Tyler Warren
players.notes: Market overvaluing by 8 picks. Waldman says not a Gronk type — more like Tucker Kraft with Michael Mayer skills. Worth 2nd half of 2nd round, not top 10.

players.name: Jack Bech
players.notes: High risk/reward.

players.name: Trey Harris
players.notes: Slight overpay.

players.name: Breshard Smith
players.notes: SLEEPER. Under 7 + High ceiling + Sleeper tag. Chiefs. Higher ceiling than Pacheco/Hunt per Waldman. Breakaway speed + receiving game.

players.name: Savion Williams
players.notes: Underrated. WR/RB hybrid. Green Bay. Bargain.
